text: Setting clear project goals is essential for success. A great way to do this is by using SMART goals, which means they are Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, and Time-bound. For example, instead of saying "finish the website," you could say "launch the new website with five completed pages by March 1st." Once you have your main goal, break it down into smaller milestones, like completing the design phase or testing the first version. This makes progress easier to track. Each milestone involves clear deliverables, such as a design mockup or a test report. Finally, ensure stakeholder alignment by discussing the goals with everyone involved—clients, team members, or managers—so you all agree on what success looks like.
